walking_skeleton_note: N/A — refactor, not feature. The end-to-end path already exists and is green (62 ingress tests).
guardrail_suite: cyrus/infra/tests/ (62 tests — behaviour-preservation, Iron Rule: no weakened/deleted assertions)
structural_suite: cyrus/infra/tests/test_clean_architecture_refactor.py (S1-S6, skip-gated, enable one at a time)
acceptance_gate: cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev pytest
constraints: One file / minimal sprawl: stay in handler.py (folding consumers.py / presence.py back in is allowed); NO new files or packages; boto3 + stdlib only; the ingress/ folder must still zip as-is.
Behaviour preserved exactly: the 62 guardrail tests stay green with no weakened or deleted assertions.
process()'s public signature is depended on by tests — any change is deliberate, called out in the PR, and the tests updated in lockstep (not loosened).
Suggested names are not load-bearing — final names at implementer discretion; the buffer use case's name must not hide its optimistic IoT probe.
In-file order after refactor: controller -> use cases -> presenter -> helpers -> composition root, named via docstrings (not inline narration); code prose is timeless (no ticket IDs).

steps.title: Extract consumer identity as a pure value object
steps.ac: S1, S2 — Identity is a pure VO
steps.intent: Lift the routing-key derivation out of _LinearConsumer.__init__ into an immutable, no-I/O value object holding the routing key (str) + is_routed (bool) ONLY — never the parsed/re-serialised body and never the raw body bytes. Identity becomes a thing the use cases USE, not something they ARE. Opaqueness is load-bearing: the signed bytes must never round-trip through the VO; the VO is derived from a COPY of the body via routing.extract_routing_key. Equality-based, hashable/frozen.
steps.verify: cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev pytest
steps.guardrail: All 62 baseline tests stay green; is_routed semantics unchanged (routed key vs _unrouted sentinel).

steps.title: Model presence as a Protocol with adapter-owned fail-closed documented
steps.intent: Define a typing.Protocol (suggested ConsumerPresenceRepository) with is_offline(username: str) -> bool. The DynamoDB-backed make_offline_check adapter satisfies it structurally (no inheritance needed). Document, in the Protocol/adapter docstring, that the adapter owns the fail-closed policy (read error -> offline) and why (iot-only has no SQS safety net, so a presence-cache blip must yield an honest 503 Linear retries, not a crash). Type the addressed-consumer use case's presence dependency as the Protocol.
steps.verify: cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev pytest
steps.guardrail: Fail-closed->503 and presence row semantics unchanged (test_presence.py + iot_only_wiring stay green, no weakened assertions).

steps.title: Separate the presenter from delivery; use cases return domain results
steps.intent: Introduce domain result types returned by the delivery logic instead of HTTP dicts: addressed-consumer -> Delivered | ConsumerOffline(username, creator_id, action); buffer -> Enqueued | PublishedUnrouted (or equivalents). Add a single presenter (suggested _shape_response(result) -> HTTPResponse) that maps results to {statusCode, body}: 200 'published'/'queued', and the honest 503 body exactly {reason: 'consumer-offline', consumer_id, action}. After this step NO statusCode/HTTP literal is produced inside any use-case function — the presenter is the sole producer. No domain type leaks into the body.
steps.verify: cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev pytest
steps.guardrail: Exact response bodies preserved (200 'published'/'queued'/'queued', 401 strings, 503 body shape) — honest-503 + opaqueness + observability guardrails stay green.

steps.title: Reframe the two strategies as named use-case functions; drop the ABC
steps.ac: S5, S6 — Named strategy use cases; delivery_mode only at the composition root
steps.intent: Replace IoTLinearConsumer.deliver / SQSLinearConsumer.deliver (and the _LinearConsumer ABC) with two use-case functions named for their single responsibility: addressed-consumer (suggested relay_webhook_event_to_consumer) — identity routing + presence check, mandatory IoT publish, honest 503 when the routed consumer is offline; buffer (suggested enqueue_webhook_event) — enqueue to the durable buffer PLUS the optimistic IoT relay probe (the name must not hide the probe). Each takes the identity VO + its ports; neither branches on delivery_mode. process() (the composition root) reads delivery_mode once, wires the chosen use case + ports, calls it, and hands the domain result to the presenter. process()'s public signature is unchanged.
steps.verify: cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev pytest
steps.guardrail: Every delivery-mode / dual-write / iot-only / offline guardrail stays green; _unrouted skips presence (no presence call, no 503); iot-only publish failure still propagates and never falls back to SQS.

steps.title: Reconcile in-file ordering, docstrings, and minimal-sprawl
steps.ac: Single file / no new ceremony; code-organization discipline
steps.intent: Order the surviving code controller -> use cases -> presenter -> helpers -> composition root, each layer NAMED by a docstring (no inline narration; timeless prose, no ticket IDs). Decide whether to fold consumers.py/presence.py back into handler.py or keep them as the existing small sibling modules — minimal-sprawl allows either, the constraint is no NEW files/packages. Confirm boto3 + stdlib only and that ingress/ still zips as-is. Remove any now-dead symbols (e.g. the old ABC).
steps.verify: cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev pytest && uv run --extra dev ruff check .
steps.guardrail: No new dependency in pyproject.toml; no new module beyond the existing flat set; full suite (62 guardrails + S1-S6) green.

steps.title: Delete the structural scaffolding tests
steps.ac: Behaviour validated by the existing suite; structural quality verified by review, not a permanent test
steps.intent: Remove cyrus/infra/tests/test_clean_architecture_refactor.py. The S1-S6 specs are throwaway DRIVERS for the refactor, not kept behavioural tests — they assert implementation SHAPE (a VO holds no body, a function has no statusCode literal, presence is a Protocol), which is brittle structure-coupling once the shape exists. The 62 existing tests are the behavioural contract and already prove behaviour is unchanged (the whole point of a move-things-around refactor); the clean-architecture SHAPE is confirmed by a refactor/architecture review of the diff, not by a standing test. After this step zero structural-spec files remain and the suite is exactly the original 62 (any genuinely new BEHAVIOUR a step introduced would instead live as a normal behavioural test, but this refactor introduces none).
steps.verify: cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev pytest  (62 passed, 0 skipped)
steps.guardrail: Net test delta is zero (no behaviour added); the structural file is gone.

final_gate: cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev pytest  (the original 62 behavioural tests green; structural scaffolding deleted; 0 skips)
cd cyrus/infra && uv run --extra dev ruff check .
Confirm ingress/ still zips as a flat boto3+stdlib asset (no new files/deps); diff is rename/restructure only
Clean-architecture SHAPE confirmed by review of the diff (e.g. nw-solution-architect-reviewer / nw-software-crafter-reviewer), not by a standing structural test
PR targets cyrus-iot-feed/release-1 (NOT main); call out any process() signature change explicitly
